title: Theresa Hak Kyung Cha’s “Perte Loss”
description: “To traverse Cha's archive is to register the force of an artistic imagination that is itself not ‘fixe, mort’ but constantly mutating.”
hook: Theresa Hak Kyung Cha's archive reveals an artist constantly shape-shifting, refusing to stay fixed or dead. A meditation on artistic mutation.

title: The Divine Powers of “Chicken Linda”
description: Pioneering performance artist Linda Mary Montano gave me a tour of her home-shrine and a glimpse into her lifelong spiritual quest through art.
hook: Linda Mary Montano gave performance art a spiritual dimension most people miss. She built a shrine to her own life and invited us inside.

title: Stephen Sondheim by Daniel Okrent review – a superb biography of the musical master
description:
Packed with gossip and incident, this book is also a fascinating study in the gestation of genius

Among the many great pleasures of Daniel Okrent’s new biography of Stephen Sondheim – a book perfectly weighted between the gossipy and erudite – is its rendering of the milieu beyond its immediate subject. You come for the biography and stay for the world of mid-20th-century New York, in which Leonard Bernstein says terrible things about Sweeney Todd (“disgusting”), Sondheim says terrible
hook: Sondheim's biography balances genius origin story with messy human gossip—how a man writes transcendent musicals while navigating loneliness and desire.

title: Tracing the Connection Between Chronic Illness and Climate Change
description:
Lorraine Boissoneault spent her twenties and thirties getting sicker. Her thyroid dysregulated. Her heart became arrhythmic. Doctors told her to manage stress and lose weight, but the diagnoses kept coming—inflamed joints, then endometriosis—and the medical advice never changed. Meanwhile, Boissoneault was working as an editor for The Weather Channel, tracking hurricanes and atmospheric changes.  After […]

The post
hook: Her body kept breaking down in ways doctors couldn't explain. Then she realized the climate was breaking down too, and maybe they were connected.

title: Jude Law Shouldn’t Be This Good as Vladimir Putin
description: He gives a transfixing performance in a new satire about the Russian president’s rise to power.
hook: Jude Law playing Putin shouldn't work, but his transfixing performance in a new satire reveals something unsettling about power and performance itself.
